content.problemStatement:
Given NNN points of the form (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi​,yi​) on a 222-D plane. Find the **maximum** number of points that lie on the same straight line.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN  — the number of points.
The next NNN lines contain two space-separated integers xix_ixi​, yiy_iyi​ — the coordinate of the ithi^{th}ith point.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the **maximum** number of points that lie on the same straight line.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of points. The next N lines each contain two space‑separated integers xi and yi, the coordinates of a point.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum number of points that lie on the same straight line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: points (3,6), (5,8), (1,4) and (12,15) are collinear, giving 4 points on the same line.\nTest case 2: both points are identical, so the line through them contains 2 points.\nTest case 3: any two points form a line, maximum is 2.

solution.approach: For each point i treat it as an anchor. For every other point j compute the direction vector (dx, dy) = (xj‑xi, yj‑yi). Reduce the vector by dividing both components by their greatest common divisor and enforce a unique sign (make dx non‑negative; if dx==0 make dy positive). Use a hash map to count how many points share the same reduced direction from i. Keep track of duplicate points that have exactly the same coordinates as i. For the current anchor the best line contains maxCount (the highest frequency in the map) + duplicates + 1 (the anchor itself). The answer for the test case is the maximum over all anchors. The algorithm runs in O(N^2) time and O(N) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2) per test case, where N ≤ 15, easily fits the limits.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the hash map used for each anchor.

code.Python3:
import sys, math

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        points = [(int(next(it)), int(next(it))) for __ in range(N)]
        best = 1
        for i in range(N):
            slopes = {}
            dup = 0
            local_max = 0
            xi, yi = points[i]
            for j in range(N):
                if i == j:
                    continue
                xj, yj = points[j]
                dx = xj - xi
                dy = yj - yi
                if dx == 0 and dy == 0:
                    dup += 1
                    continue
                g = math.gcd(dx, dy)
                dx //= g
                dy //= g
                if dx < 0 or (dx == 0 and dy < 0):
                    dx = -dx
                    dy = -dy
                cnt = slopes.get((dx, dy), 0) + 1
                slopes[(dx, dy)] = cnt
                if cnt > local_max:
                    local_max = cnt
            best = max(best, local_max + dup + 1)
        out_lines.append(str(best))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
